zh.lifeSummary: 當James Collier於1490年出生在法國哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯斯頓時，他的父親Robert Collier37歲，母親Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )30歲。他和Isabella Leveson至少有6個兒子和10個女兒。他去世於1548年3月30日在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾史東享年58歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Collier于1490年出生在法国哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯斯頓时，他的父亲Robert Collier37岁，母亲Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )30岁。他和Isabella Leveson至少有6个儿子和10个女儿。他去世于1548年3月30日在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾史東享年58岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Collier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English colier, in most parts of the country ‘maker or seller of charcoal’, but in some areas (such as Bolton le Moors and Wigan, Lancashire) where coal measures were near the surface, ‘miner or seller of coal’ (in the modern sense, ‘fossil fuel’). The name was taken to Ireland from England and was first recorded there in 1305. In Petty's ‘census’ of 1659, it was recorded as a principal surname in Meath.
English: occupational name from Middle English coilour, coliour, culliour, Old French coileor, coillour ‘tax collector’. Surnames with this origin seem to have died out in Britain.
French (northern): from collier ‘collar’, a metonymic occupational name for a maker of collars.

variants.name: Callier
meanings: French: from an Old French term denoting earthenware and, by extension, a particular type of earthenware vessel from which new wine was drunk, hence probably a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such crocks.
French: from caielier, an occupational name for a chair maker, from an agent derivative of caiel ‘chair’.

English (Staffordshire): probably a variant of the occupational name Collier ‘charcoal burner’, though in some cases perhaps a variant of Caller, from a Middle English side-form callier with substitution of the suffix -ier for -er. In Kent and Somerset, Caller is chiefly a variant of the nickname or habitational name Callow . Elsewhere it may derive either from Middle English caller ‘maker of cauls’ (baskets, nets, women's close-fitting caps or headdresses for covering tied up hair), from Middle English calle ‘basket, net’ (Old English cawl), or from a topographic or occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a sheep pen or pigsty (Middle English calle), synonymous with atte Calle. See Call .

variants.name: Coller
meanings: Altered form of Swiss German and German   Koller   or   Kohler  .

variants.name: Collar
meanings: Spanish: from collar ‘collar’. This name is found especially in Asturias and Extremadura.
Americanized form of German   Koller   or   Kohler  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Collier was born in 1490, in Stonne, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France, his father, Robert Collier, was 37 and his mother, Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ), was 30. He had at least 6 sons and 10 daughters with Isabella Leveson. He died on 30 March 1548, in Stone, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Collier est né en 1490, à Stonne, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France, son père, Robert Collier, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ), avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 10 filles avec Isabella Leveson. Il est décédé le 30 mars 1548, à Stone, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Collier 1490 in Stonne, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Collier 37 und seine Mutter Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) 30. Er hatte mit Isabella Leveson mindestens 6 Söhne und 10 Töchter. Er starb am 30. März 1548 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Stone, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Collier è nato il nel 1490 nel Stonne, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, il suo padre, Robert Collier, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ), aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 10 figlie con Isabella Leveson. Morì il 30 marzo 1548, nel Stone, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: James Collierが1490年にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアルデンスストンで生まれた時，父Robert Collierは37歳，母Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )は30歳であった。彼はIsabella Levesonとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と10人の娘をもうけた。彼は1548年3月30日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアストーンで58歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Collier(이)가 1490년 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 아데네스 스토니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Collier(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Isabella Leveson(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1548년 3월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 스톤에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Collier nasceu em 1490, em Stonne, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, França, seu pai, Robert Collier, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ), tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 10 filhas com Isabella Leveson. Ele faleceu em 30 de março de 1548, em Stone, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Collier родился в 1490 г. в местности стонн, арденнесь, чампагне-арденн, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert Collier было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ), 30. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 10 дочерей от Isabella Leveson. Он умер 30 марта 1548 г. в местности стоун, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности стаффордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Collier nació en 1490, en Stonne, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, su papá, Robert Collier, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ), tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 10 hijas con Isabella Leveson. Murió el 30 de marzo de 1548, en Stone, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
